data.title: Nike to Lay Off 1,400 Workers in Latest Round of Layoffs — WSJ
data.description:
By Inti Pacheco

Nike will reduce its workforce by some 1,400 workers, or about 2%, as part of the company's plan to simplify the complexity in its global operations.

The reduction will mostly affect employees in Nike's technology department, the company said Thursday.

"This is not a new direction. It is the next phase of the work already underway," said Chief Operations Officer Venkatesh Alagirisamy in a note to employees.

The layoffs come as the sneaker giant works through the turnaround plan laid out by Nike chief executive Elliott Hill. The company cut about 800 workers in January and it said in March it expected to record a $300 million charge related to cost-cutting efforts.

Write to Inti Pacheco at inti.pacheco@wsj.com

data.title: First Financial Bancorp Gets Stock Buyback Authorization for Up to 5M Shares
data.description:
By Elias Schisgall

First Financial Bancorp's board authorized a stock buyback plan for up to 5 million shares.

The authorization represents around 4.8% of the company's outstanding shares as of March 31, the company said.

The plan was effective on Tuesday and expires at the end of 2027.

It replaces a previous stock buyback program which expired at the end of last year.

Write to Elias Schisgall at elias.schisgall@wsj.com

data.title: Mexico opens probe into economy minister over son's stay in diplomatic residence
data.description:
By Ana Isabel Martinez and Raul Cortes

Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum said on Thursday the government has opened an investigation into Economy Minister Marcelo Ebrard to determine whether he violated any laws when he sent his adult son to live at a diplomatic residence in the United Kingdom.

Ebrard acknowledged last week that his son spent six months living at the Mexican ambassador's residence in London while studying between 2021 and 2022, but denied his son used public funds. Ebrard, who was then Mexico's foreign minister, dismissed any wrongdoing on his part, beyond “a father’s concern for his son."

During his stay at the ambassador's residence, Ebrard's son had a butler, a cook and cleaning staff at his disposal, according to El Pais newspaper.

Sheinbaum said on Thursday at her daily morning press conference that complaints had been filed with the anti-corruption and good governance minister, triggering an automatic investigation. She added the agency will determine whether any regulations were violated.

The investigation could spark tensions between the president and Ebrard, who is leading Mexico's negotiations with the United States and Canada to renew a trilateral trade agreement pivotal to Mexico's economy.

Ebrard and Sheinbaum have had a complicated relationship.

They were rivals in the race for the ruling Morena Party’s presidential nomination in the 2024 elections. At the time, Ebrard accused Sheinbaum’s campaign of busing in people to her rallies, releasing fake polls and waging a smear campaign against him.

After winning the election and taking office, Sheinbaum named Ebrard economy minister, citing his experience in negotiations, particularly with Washington, and his ties to the private sector.

data.title: Cattle futures rise on bargain buying after six-session slide
data.description:
By Karl Plume

U.S. cattle futures rebounded on Thursday on technical support and bargain buying after six straight sessions of declines, traders said.

Weaker cash cattle sales at Plains feedlot markets this week and some questions about beef demand resilience amid historically high prices and tightening consumer budgets capped gains, they said.

Chicago Mercantile Exchange June live cattle (LCM26) settled 0.425 cent higher at 243.500 cents per pound after sinking to their lowest level since March 31 in the previous session. May feeder cattle (FCK26), which reached their lowest level since March 27 at midweek, ended 0.450 cent higher at 358.875 cents per pound.

"It's been a big sell-off and I think people think it's gone far enough. That's why I think some guys stepped in and did some bargain buying today," said independent livestock trader Dan Norcini.

Limited cash market sales at Plains feedlot markets were $2 per hundredweight below last week's traded prices as beef packers grappled with a recent drop in processing margins and lighter cattle slaughter rates.

The U.S. Department of Agriculture estimated Thursday's cattle slaughter at 106,000 head, down from 116,598 head a year ago. Traders said a beef plant in Fort Morgan, Colorado, would not slaughter cattle on Thursday and Friday in the latest processing disruption following a weeks-long strike at a plant in Greeley, Colorado, last month.

The average beef packer margin fell to a negative $188.60 per head on Thursday, from a negative $167.00 per head a day earlier, according to livestock marketing advisory service HedgersEdge.

CME lean hog futures also rose on Thursday, supported by bargain buying after a recent steep decline and speculative buying as traders anticipated a seasonal increase in prices.

Benchmark June lean hogs (LHM26) ended 0.825 cent higher at 103.450 cents per pound.

data.title: HAIVISION ANNOUNCES VOTING RESULTS FROM 2026 ANNUAL MEETING OF SHAREHOLDERS
data.description:
MONTRÉAL, April 23, 2026 /PRNewswire/ - Haivision Systems Inc. ("Haivision" or the "Company") is pleased to announce the voting results from its annual meeting of shareholders held today in a virtual format.

A total of approximately 45.97 % of the issued and outstanding common shares of Haivision were represented at the meeting.

1. Election of Directors

Each of the six nominated directors of Haivision was elected as director of the Company with the following results:

 
                        Votes     % Votes    Votes    % Votes 
      Director            For       For     Against    Against 
--------------------  ----------  -------  ---------  -------- 
 Miroslav Wicha       11,110,245  99.26 %   82,583     0.74 % 
 Harvey Bienenstock   11,155,137  99.66 %   37,691     0.34 % 
 Robin M. Rush        11,121,855  99.37 %   70,973     0.63 % 
 Neil Hindle          10,794,005  96.44 %   398,823    3.56 % 
 Julie Tremblay       10,941,969  97.76 %   250,859    2.24 % 
 Lee K. Levy II       9,084,418   81.16 %  2,108,410  18.84 %

2. Appointment of Auditors

Deloitte LLP were reappointed auditors of the Company for the ensuing year with 12,492,582 (98.84%) votes cast in favour and 146,406 (1.16%) votes withheld.

3. Approval of the Unallocated Awards under the Company's Equity Incentive Plan

The Company's unallocated awards were approved with 8,710,347 (77.82%) votes cast in favour and 2,482,481 (22.18%) votes cast against.

4. Reapproval of Company's Shareholder Rights Plan

The Company's shareholder rights plan was approved with 10,572,490 (94.46%) votes cast in favour and 620,338 (5.54%) votes cast against.

Final voting results on all matters voted on at the meeting will be filed under Haivision's profile on SEDAR+ at www.sedarplus.ca.

About Haivision

Haivision is a leading global provider of mission-critical, real-time video streaming and visual collaboration solutions. Our connected cloud and intelligent edge technologies enable organizations globally to engage audiences, enhance collaboration, and support decision making. We provide high quality, low latency, secure, and reliable live video at a global scale. Haivision open sourced its award-winning SRT low latency video streaming protocol and founded the SRT Alliance to support its adoption. Awarded four Emmys(R) for Technology and Engineering from the National Academy of Television Arts and Sciences, Haivision continues to fuel the future of IP video transformation. Founded in 2004, Haivision is headquartered in Montreal and Chicago with offices, sales, and support located throughout the Americas, Europe, and Asia. Learn more at haivision.com.

View original content to download multimedia:https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/haivision-announces-voting-results-from-2026-annual-meeting-of-shareholders-302752318.html

SOURCE Haivision Systems Inc.

data.title: Camber Energy Subsidiary Raises $500,000 10% Note Secured by VKIN-300 Sale Proceeds
data.description:
Camber Energy's subsidiary Viking Ozone Technology entered into a secured promissory note with an accredited investor for $500,000 at a 10% fixed rate. The note matures on the earlier of Apr 15, 2027 or receipt of proceeds from the unconditional sale of the VKIN-300 waste treatment unit, and may be part of a series totaling up to $750,000. As security, the investor holds a pari passu priority interest over VOT's net proceeds from the unit sale. The note has customary default terms and includes no conversion rights or warrants; Camber Energy is not a party to the note.

Agreement details:

  • Agreement type: Secured promissory note
  • Counterparty: Accredited investor
  • Signed / Effective: Apr 17 2026 / same
  • Duration / Termination: Until earlier of Apr 15 2027 or Unit Sale proceeds
  • Reason: Provide funding secured by VKIN-300 unit sale proceeds

data.title: SAP Stock Rebounds From Software Slaughter. The Cloud Boosted Europe's Tech Titan. — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Kit Norton

Shares of SAP took off after the closing bell on Thursday after the German tech company reported better-than-expected first-quarter profit and reiterated its 2026 cloud revenue outlook, with a caveat.

SAP's American depositary receipts advanced 6% after the closing bell on Thursday, mostly reversing a 6.2% decline to $163.25 in regular trading.

Software stocks were hit hard on Thursday as investors were quick to sell off the embattled sector following solid but unspectacular earnings reports from ServiceNow and IBM.

SAP, the owner of the Concur expense and travel management platform, reported first-quarter earnings of EUR1.72 ($2.01) a share, as revenue rose 6% from a year ago to EUR9.55 billion. Those are the set of figures that aren't based on International Financial Reporting Standards (IFRS), meaning they exclude one-off or unusual items such as restructuring costs or amortization.

Analysts were expecting non-IFRS earnings of EUR1.65 a share on revenue of EUR9.55 billion, according to a FactSet poll.

SAP's all-important cloud unit, which has benefited in recent years from the artificial-intelligence boom, logged revenue of EUR5.96 billion, up 19% from a year ago and coming in just above the EUR5.89 billion Wall Street was expecting.

The tech company added that it ended the first quarter with a cloud backlog of EUR21.9 billion, up 20% compared with a year ago.

"This performance is supported by our momentum in Business AI as we are already delivering real outcomes for customers today. We are growing faster than the market and are gaining share as customers expand across our Suite and with our AI solutions," CEO Christian Klein said in the earnings release.

The company continues to expect 2026 cloud revenue of between EUR25.8 billion and EUR26.2 billion. At the midpoint, that's just above the EUR25.62 billion that analysts had been forecasting. However, SAP said this guidance is "based on the assumption of a near-term de-escalation of the conflict in the Middle East and the imminent consolidation of Reltio."

In late March, SAP announced it has agreed to acquire Reltio, a data management software provider, with the transaction expected to close in the second or third quarter.

SAP added Thursday that it also forecasts total revenue growth, in constant currencies, in 2026 to remain at similar levels as in 2025 but that it will "accelerate" in 2027.

SAP is one of Europe's biggest tech stocks, but with a market capitalization of $204.98 billion, it's a fraction of Microsoft's $3.21 trillion market cap.

Write to Kit Norton at kit.norton@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Blackstone Stock Falls After Earnings. Private Credit Remains in Focus. — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Mackenzie Tatananni

Blackstone stock traded down on Thursday as investors weighed a beat in overall March quarter earnings, against falling returns within its large private credit business. The world's largest private asset manager had another quarter of successful fundraising.

Shares in the private asset manager slipped 4.7% Thursday morning, to $123.60, against a 0.2% drop in the S&P 500. Blackstone leaders boasted of the firm's huge bets on data centers and energy for artificial intelligence, while predicting that wealthy investors' redemptions from private credit funds would subside.

Blackstone CEO Stephen Schwarzman told conference call listeners that he had been involved in AI since 2015, long before the rise of today's large language models from OpenAI and Anthropic.

The firm said it is the largest investor in AI data centers, which need far more capital than public markets can provide.

"This strategic decision that we made to go long AI infrastructure is going to be the single most important thing for the performance of our clients and ultimately, the growth of our business," said Blackstone President Jonathan Gray.

Distributable earnings jumped 25% in the March quarter, to $1.76 billion, or $1.36 a share — passing the $1.34 a share analysts had anticipated. The measure represents the cash flow available to be paid as dividends to shareholders.

Total revenue increased 10% to $3.62 billion. Blackstone finished the quarter with a staggering $1.3 trillion in assets under management, a 12% increase. But returns fell across certain businesses, even as Blackstone drew in $68.5 billion in new assets.

Its private credit and insurance business accounted for nearly half of at that, with inflows of $37 billion in the quarter. The division's returns were among the lowest ever reported: Blackstone's private credit funds delivered zero net returns on average after fees, while its portfolio of bank loans lost 1.4%. Distributable earnings in the division fell 26% to $373 million.

But Gray said that newspapers' anxious headlines about private credit were sharply out of line with the long-term performance of the strategy. The firm's $86 billion non-traded Blackstone Private Credit Fund, known as BCRED, has returned 9.4% since it started five years ago, and has handily outperformed more liquid credit investment categories.

Amid a rush of redemptions by wealthy investors across non-traded credit funds, shareholders representing some 7% of BCRED's $47 billion in net assets cashed out in March. Gray said that they represented a small number of relatively large investors, while the vast majority of wealthy investors stayed put.

And insurers and pension funds have continued to put money into private credit, in sharp contrast to individuals, Gray said. He predicted that redemptions by BCRED's individual investors would subside, as they did a year ago at the firm's real estate fund BREIT.

Infrastructure investments, a category including data centers and related artificial-intelligence infrastructure, recorded notably strong performance. Blackstone's infrastructure arm posted gross returns of 7.8% in the quarter and nearly 25% over the past 12 months.

Write to Mackenzie Tatananni at mackenzie.tatananni@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Baker Hughes 1Q Profit, Revenue Rise on Industrial and Energy Technology Growth
data.description:
By Kelly Cloonan

Baker Hughes logged higher profit and revenue in its latest quarter as sales in its industrial and energy technology unit grew.

The energy technology company on Thursday posted a first-quarter profit of $930 million, or 93 cents a share, compared with $402 million, or 40 cents a share, a year earlier.

Adjusted earnings per share were 58 cents, compared with estimates of 49 cents a share according to analysts polled by FactSet.

Revenue rose 2% to $6.59 billion, compared with analyst estimates of $6.34 billion.

The company's industrial and energy technology unit posted sales of $3.35 billion, up 14%. Sales in the company's oilfield services and equipment segment declined 7%, to $3.24 billion.

Overall orders in the quarter were $8.16 billion, up 26% from a year ago.

Chief Executive Lorenzo Simonelli said the company contended with significant disruptions in the Middle East during the quarter.

"Looking ahead, our outlook for the business fundamentals remains unchanged, excluding the ongoing impacts in the Middle East," Simonelli said, noting the conflict presents near-term challenges but is reinforcing energy security as a priority, which should support growth in upstream and global energy infrastructure spending.

Write to Kelly Cloonan at kelly.cloonan@wsj.com

data.title: Insurer Erie Indemnity's profit rises on higher investment income
data.description:
Property and casualty insurer Erie Indemnity reported a rise in first-quarter profit on Thursday, helped by strong growth in investment income and fees.

Insurance demand remains resilient, underpinned by the essential nature of coverage that protects households and businesses from large and unpredictable losses, even as economic uncertainty driven by the U.S.-Israeli war on Iran is expected to fuel inflation.

Investment income also has stayed robust for insurers even as market volatility and economic uncertainty test investor sentiment. Insurers typically invest a portion of their capital to various asset classes, including fixed-income securities and equities.

Here are some details:

  • Erie's net income rose to $150.5 million, or $2.88 per diluted share, in the three months ended March 31, compared to $138.4 million, or $2.65 per diluted share, in the year-ago period.

  • Its income from investments before taxes climbed nearly 13.2% in the quarter.

  • The company's management fee revenue from policy issuance and renewal services increased 4.2% to $786.4 million.

  • Meanwhile, fee revenue from administrative services rose 10.4% to $19.5 million.

  • Erie Indemnity's stock has fallen 13.2% so far this year.

data.title: Oceaneering International Q1: Revenue $692M, EPS $0.36
data.description:
Oceaneering International reported first-quarter 2026 revenue of $692 million, up 3% year‑over‑year, while diluted earnings per share fell to $0.36 from $0.49 a year earlier. The company cited lower gross margin and a 21% decline in operating income that together pressured net income to $36.1 million. Management noted shifts in activity across its ADTech and Subsea Robotics businesses that affected segment utilization and mix.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue: $692 million (Q1 2026 $692,429; Q1 2025 $674,523), a 3% increase versus prior year.
  • Gross margin: $127.3 million (Q1 2026 $127,270 vs Q1 2025 $135,011), representing a margin decline year over year.
  • Income (loss) from operations: $57.8 million (Q1 2026 $57,788; Q1 2025 $73,472), down 21% versus prior year.
  • Net income (loss): $36.1 million (Q1 2026 $36,107; Q1 2025 $50,377), decreased from prior year.
  • Diluted net income per share: $0.36 (Q1 2026 $0.36; Q1 2025 $0.49), down from $0.49 a year earlier.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue mix and segment activity: Consolidated revenue growth was driven by pricing improvements in ADTech and Subsea Robotics, while ADTech activity increased materially.
  • Subsea Robotics utilization: Lower ROV utilization at 61% versus 67% in the prior-year period shifted the company’s revenue mix toward higher ADTech activity.
  • Manufactured Products: Margins improved on execution of higher‑margin backlog for manufactured product lines.
  • Fleet and vessel strategy: The company maintained a 250 work‑class ROV fleet and is pursuing a diversified vessel strategy combining short‑term, spot charters and five long‑term charters to better align capacity with demand.
  • Regional performance and outlook: IMDS performance was pressured by the Middle East conflict and reduced activity in Africa and Australia; management is targeting growth in integrity, survey, robotics and offshore renewables.

Original SEC Filing: OCEANEERING INTERNATIONAL INC [ OII ] - 10-Q - Apr. 23, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Enova posts $875.1M revenue and $3.46 EPS; Q — strong origination-driven growth
data.description:
Enova reported consolidated revenue of $875.1 million for the period, up 17.4% year-over-year, driven by higher originations and portfolio growth, and delivered diluted earnings per share of $3.46 (net income $91.1 million). The company also reported operating income of $207.1 million, reflecting improved operating margins and efficiency. Management highlighted continued momentum across consumer and small-business channels and expanded securitization capacity to support origination growth.

Financial Highlights

  • Total Revenue: $875.1 million, up $129.6 million (17.4%) year-over-year.
  • Net Revenue: $529.0 million, up $102.8 million (24.1%); net revenue margin 60.4% (improved versus 57.2%).
  • Operating Income: $207.1 million, up $35.2 million (20.5%); operating margin approximately 23.6%.
  • Net Income: $91.1 million, up $18.2 million (24.9%) year-over-year.
  • Diluted Net Income Per Share: $3.46 (prior year: $2.69).

Business Highlights

  • Revenue drivers: Growth was driven by higher originations and expanding balances across both consumer and small-business portfolios.
  • Channel & product mix: A shift toward larger small-business originations increased average loan size and moved the portfolio mix to about 70% small business by fair value.
  • Brand momentum: Continued growth across CashNetUSA, NetCredit, OnDeck, Headway Capital and Pangea money-transfer, with expansion in Brazil through Simplic.
  • Operational scaling: Increased securitization capacity to support origination growth and processed approximately 4.3 million transactions during the period (about 70.4 million lifetime transactions).
  • Technology & analytics: Heavy reliance on machine learning and AI—approximately 90% of models—and proprietary underwriting to accelerate decisions, refine credit models and improve repeat-customer performance.

Original SEC Filing: Enova International, Inc. [ ENVA ] - 10-Q - Apr. 23, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Canada's Westport Fuel Q4 revenue falls sharply 74%, warns on liquidity

data.title: OP Bancorp Boosts Quarterly Dividend by 17%
data.description:
By Christopher Kuo

OP Bancorp said it has raised its quarterly cash dividend by 17%.

This increases the dividend to 14 cents a share from 12 cents.

The new payout of 56 cents a year represents an annual yield of about 4% based on Thursday's closing price of $14.11.

The company, which is a holding company for Open Bank, said on Thursday that the dividend is payable on or about May 21 to shareholders of record as of the close of business May 7.

Write to Christopher Kuo at chris.kuo@wsj.com

data.title: Investcorp Credit Management BDC posts $172.7M portfolio FMV; 81% senior secured debt, 9.1% weighted coupon
data.description:
Investcorp Credit Management BDC reported a portfolio FMV of $172.7 million with ~81% in senior secured debt and a weighted-average coupon of 9.1%.

Key Highlights:

  • Portfolio FMV $172.7M across 37 companies, 67 positions in 18 industries; top 10 holdings FMV $85.3M.
  • Approximately 80.8% of portfolio FMV is senior secured (1L) debt, giving capital-structure priority.
  • Weighted-average coupon (cash+PIK+base) on debt is 9.1%; ~20% of FMV has a PIK component.
  • Weighted-average net leverage 5.2x with interest coverage ratio 2.2x and portfolio LTV 46.3%.
  • About 90% of FMV carries Risk Ratings 2 or 3; non-accrual positions represent 6.9% of the portfolio.

data.title: Primis Financial Q1 operating net income more than doubles year-over-year
data.description:
Overview

  • U.S. regional bank's Q1 operating net income more than doubled year-over-year

  • GAAP net income and EPS fell from prior year

  • Company declared a dividend at $0.10 per share

Outlook

  • Company expects robust profitability in 2026 as it continues to execute its operating plan

  • Primis anticipates annual noninterest income to improve by about $1.2 mln from life insurance restructuring starting late Q2 2026

  • Company expects core Bank SBA gain on sale income to rise to $500,000-$600,000 per quarter starting Q2 2026

Result Drivers

  • NET INTEREST INCOME - Net interest income rose 22% year-over-year, driven by growth in earning assets and improved net interest margin

  • MORTGAGE AND WAREHOUSE LENDING - Mortgage banking income nearly doubled and mortgage warehouse lending balances increased sharply, boosting noninterest income

  • COST CONTROL AND TECHNOLOGY - Company said it is implementing AI tools to improve productivity and manage expenses, with core operating expenses flat excluding sale-leaseback impact

data.title: Greencastle Announces Closing of Acquisition of Common Shares of Future Fuels Inc.
data.description:
Toronto, Ontario--(Newsfile Corp. - April 23, 2026) - Greencastle Resources Ltd. ("Greencastle" or the "Company") is pleased to announce that it has closed its previously announced acquisition (the "Acquisition") of an aggregate of 500,000 common shares (the "Purchased Shares") in the capital of Future Fuels Inc. ("Future Fuels") pursuant to a share purchase agreement dated April 13, 2026 with an arm's length third party (the "Vendor"). Prior to the acquisition of the Purchased Shares, the Company held 480,000 common shares of Future Fuels.

As consideration for the Purchased Shares, the Company issued 4,600,000 common shares (the "Consideration Shares") from treasury at a deemed price of $0.05 per share for an aggregate deemed value of $230,000. No cash consideration was paid.

The Acquisition was completed for investment purposes and is consistent with the Company's strategy to pursue selective positions in prospective resource companies and projects. Future Fuels is engaged in the resource sector, and the Company believes that the Acquisition provides attractive exposure to potential commodity-cycle upside and complements the Company's broader portfolio focus.

Closing of the Acquisition has occurred following receipt of all necessary corporate approvals and acceptance of the TSX Venture Exchange (the "TSXV") for the issuance of the Consideration Shares.

The Consideration Shares were issued under applicable Canadian securities laws and are subject to a statutory hold period of four months and one day from the date of issuance, expiring on August 24, 2026. The Consideration Shares are expected to be listed for trading on the TSXV upon expiry of the hold period, subject to TSXV acceptance and compliance with applicable listing requirements.

The Vendor is arm's length to the Company within the meaning of applicable securities laws. No finder's fees or commissions were paid in connection with the Acquisition. The Acquisition does not constitute a "related party transaction" under Multilateral Instrument 61-101 - Protection of Minority Security Holders in Special Transactions.

For additional information, please visit www.greencastle.ltd or contact:

Albert Contardi

Chief Executive Officer

Tel.: 416-361-2832

Notice regarding Forward-Looking Information

Neither TSX Venture Exchange nor its Regulation Services Provider (as that term is defined in the policies of the TSX Venture Exchange) accepts responsibility for the adequacy or accuracy of this release.

Disclaimer

This news release contains "forward-looking information" and "forward-looking statements" (collectively, "forward-looking statements") within the meaning of the applicable Canadian securities legislation. All statements, other than statements of historical fact, are forward-looking statements and are based on expectations, estimates and projections as at the date of this news release. Any statement that involves discussions with respect to predictions, expectations, beliefs, plans, projections, objectives, assumptions, future events or performance (often but not always using phrases such as "expects", or "does not expect", "is expected", "anticipates" or "does not anticipate", "plans", "budget", "scheduled", "forecasts", "estimates", "believes" or "intends" or variations of such words and phrases or stating that certain actions, events or results "may" or "could", "would", "might" or "will" be taken to occur or be achieved) are not statements of historical fact and may be forward-looking statements. These forward-looking statements are subject to a variety of risks and uncertainties and other factors that could cause actual events or results to differ materially from those projected in the forward-looking information. TORONTO, April 23, 2026 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Martinrea International Inc. today announced it will report its financial results for the first quarter ended March 31, 2026 on Thursday, April 30, 2026 at 4:30 p.m. Eastern Time. The Company will hold a conference call to discuss the results the same day at 5:30 p.m. Eastern Time. To participate, please dial 416-855-9085 (Toronto area) or 800-990-2777 (toll free Canada and US) and enter participant code 16073#. Please call 10 minutes prior to the start of the conference call.

The conference call will also be webcast live in listen‐only mode and archived for twelve months. The webcast and accompanying presentation can be accessed at: https://www.martinrea.com/investor-relations/events-presentations/

data.title: Paycom Software Gets $2.125B Credit Facility From JPMorgan
data.description:
By Elias Schisgall

Paycom Software entered an amended credit agreement for a $2.125 billion revolving credit facility with JPMorgan Chase.

The agreement allows the cloud-based, human-capital-management software company to request an incremental facility of up to $750 million, the company said Thursday.

All loans under the agreement will mature on April 23, 2031.

As of Thursday, the principal amount outstanding under Paycom's revolving credit facility was about $675 million.

The company said it may use the borrowings for working capital and general corporate purposes, including permitted acquisitions and share repurchases.

Write to Elias Schisgall at elias.schisgall@wsj.com

data.title: Soft Serve: Heard on the Street Recap — WSJ
data.description:
By Heard Editors

What Happened in Markets Today

Escalation fears weigh on stocks. Another U.S. aircraft carrier arrived in the Middle East, while President Trump said he has ordered the navy to "shoot and kill" any boat laying mines in the Strait of Hormuz. Oil prices rose for the fourth consecutive session.

Major indexes slip. The S&P 500 dropped 0.4% for the day while the Nasdaq slid nearly 1%. The Dow fell 180 points to 49,310. Microsoft, the third-largest component on the price-weighted index, slid 4% despite news that the company is trying to trim its workforce by 7% by offering voluntary buyouts.

Software stocks crash. Poorly received quarterly reports from ServiceNow and IBM weighed on the group. ServiceNow shares slid nearly 18% while IBM's dropped more than 8%. The iShares Expanded Tech-Software Sector ETF that tracks many key software players was down 6% for the day.

Chips break from the tech pack. Most chip stocks rose Thursday, led by an 19% surge by Texas Instruments following its own quarterly results. The PHLX Semiconductor Index picked up 1.7%.

This analysis comes from the Journal's Heard on the Street team. Subscribe to their free daily afternoon newsletter here.

This item is part of a Wall Street Journal live coverage event. The full stream can be found by searching P/WSJL (WSJ Live Coverage).
